title: City life and urban problems
explanation:
City life and urban problems

  • housing, traffic jam, public transport, noise pollution, overcrowding
  • pedestrian area, cycle lane, suburb, city centre
  • verbs: commute, expand, improve, reduce

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Heavy ____ can make a short journey take an hour. | *traffic | evidence | income
Many people move to the ____ because housing is cheaper there. | *suburbs | runways | theories
The council wants to create more ____ so cyclists can travel safely. | *cycle lanes | luggage belts | deadlines
Living in a huge city often means dealing with ____. | *overcrowding | scholarships | revisions
questions.feedback: Too many people in one place.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: She has to ____ for ninety minutes every day. | commute
The city plans to ____ the tram network next year. | expand
A new law could help ____ noise pollution at night. | reduce
Many tourists prefer to stay in the city ____.  | centre
Better buses would ____ daily life for residents. | improve

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: This street is a ____ area, so cars cannot enter. | *pedestrian | volunteer | broadcast
Young adults often struggle with high ____ costs. | *housing | platform | weather
Good ____ is essential in large cities. | *public transport | salary | furniture
Air ____ is worse near busy roads. | *pollution | promotion | discussion
